text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Children's Rights Groups 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF NATIONAL INDIAN CHILD WELFARE ASSOCIATION CHILD ABUSE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral for children and families of Native American descent regarding issues of child abuse, custody, termination of parental rights, adoption, and foster care. Works to address the issues of child abuse and neglect through training, research, public policy, and grassroots community development. Also works to support compliance with the Indian Child Welfare Act of 1978. This act seeks to keep Native American children with Native American families. Does not provide legal advice or case management services. NICWA is not a direct service provider. 5100 S Macadam Avenue Suite 300 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 222-4044 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR CHILDREN OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Advocates for children by developing coalitions with policymakers to help prevent child abuse. PO Box 14694 Portland, OR 97293 Eligibility: Ages 0-17 Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 236-9754 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL INDIAN CHILD WELFARE ASSOCIATION CHILD ABUSE SERVICES OUR CHILDREN OREGON OUR CHILDREN OREGON
